initial_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_,C,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Safe predicates for arithmetic (appear positively)
row(R) :- true.
col(C) :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Identify the unique core row (the row that is entirely colour 5)
coreRow(Rc) :- true, #count{ C : input(Rc,C,5) } = n_cols(M).

% Identify the centre column of the board
coreCol(Cc) :- true, Cc = n_cols(N) / 2.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Keep the colour 5 cells of the core row
output(Rc,C,5) :- coreRow(Rc), input(Rc,C,5).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Colour 2  copy to the two rows that are one step closer to the core
% (keep the original cell)
output(R,C,2) :- input(R,C,2).

% copies above the core row (R < coreRow)
output(R1,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R + 1,
                     R1 < coreRow(Rc), row(R1), col(C1),
                     (C < coreCol(Cc)  C1 = C - 1 : C1 = C).
output(R2,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R2 = R + 2,
                     R2 < coreRow(Rc), row(R2), col(C2),
                     (C < coreCol(Cc)  C2 = C - 1 : C2 = C).

% copies below the core row (R > coreRow)
output(R1,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R - 1,
                     R1 > coreRow(Rc), col(C1), C1 = C.
output(R2,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R2 = R - 2,
                     R2 > coreRow(Rc), col(C2), C2 = C.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Colour 1  above core row: shift to column coreCol+1
output(R,C,1) :- input(R,C,1), coreRow(Rc), R < coreRow(Rc),
                 (C <= coreCol(Cc)  C1 = coreCol(Cc) + 1 : C1 = C).
output(R1,C1,1) :- input(R,C,1), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R + 1,
                     R1 < coreRow(Rc), (C <= coreCol(Cc)  C1 = coreCol(Cc) + 1 : C1 = C).

% below core row: shift left by one column
output(R1,C1,1) :- input(R,C,1), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R - 1,
                     R1 > coreRow(Rc), C > 0, C1 = C - 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Prohibit colours 2 and 1 inside the core row
:- output(R,C,2), coreRow(R).
:- output(R,C,1), coreRow(R).

% -------------------------------------------------
% No colour 5 outside the core row (counting based)
count_5cells       :- #count{ (R,C,Color) : output(R,C,5) }.
count_core5cells   :- #count{ (R,C) : coreRow(R), output(R,C,5) }.
:- count_5cells = count_core5cells.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Counting to force zeros on all nonforced cells
forced(R,C) :- output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
count_nonzero    :- #count{ (R,C,Color) : output(R,C,Color), Color != 0 }.
count_forced     :- #count{ (R,C) : forced(R,C) }.
:- count_nonzero = count_forced.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- input(R,C,_).

% -------------------------------------------------
#show output/3.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_,C,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Safe predicates for arithmetic (appear positively)
row(R) :- true.
col(C) :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Identify the unique core row (the row that is entirely colour 5)
coreRow(Rc) :- true, #count{ C : input(Rc,C,5) } = n_cols(M).

% Identify the centre column of the board
coreCol(Cc) :- true, Cc = n_cols(N) / 2.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Keep the colour 5 cells of the core row
output(Rc,C,5) :- coreRow(Rc), input(Rc,C,5).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Colour 2  copy to the two rows that are one step closer to the core
% (keep the original cell)
output(R,C,2) :- input(R,C,2).

% copies above the core row (R < coreRow)
output(R1,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R + 1,
                     R1 < coreRow(Rc), row(R1), col(C1),
                     (C < coreCol(Cc)  C1 = C - 1 : C1 = C).
output(R2,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R2 = R + 2,
                     R2 < coreRow(Rc), row(R2), col(C2),
                     (C < coreCol(Cc)  C2 = C - 1 : C2 = C).

% copies below the core row (R > coreRow)
output(R1,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R - 1,
                     R1 > coreRow(Rc), col(C1), C1 = C.
output(R2,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R2 = R - 2,
                     R2 > coreRow(Rc), col(C2), C2 = C.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Colour 1  above core row: shift to column coreCol+1
output(R,C,1) :- input(R,C,1), coreRow(Rc), R < coreRow(Rc),
                 (C <= coreCol(Cc)  C1 = coreCol(Cc) + 1 : C1 = C).
output(R1,C1,1) :- input(R,C,1), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R + 1,
                     R1 < coreRow(Rc), (C <= coreCol(Cc)  C1 = coreCol(Cc) + 1 : C1 = C).

% below core row: shift left by one column
output(R1,C1,1) :- input(R,C,1), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R - 1,
                     R1 > coreRow(Rc), C > 0, C1 = C - 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Prohibit colours 2 and 1 inside the core row
:- output(R,C,2), coreRow(R).
:- output(R,C,1), coreRow(R).

% -------------------------------------------------
% No colour 5 outside the core row (counting based)
count_5cells       :- #count{ (R,C,Color) : output(R,C,5) }.
count_core5cells   :- #count{ (R,C) : coreRow(R), output(R,C,5) }.
:- count_5cells = count_core5cells.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Counting to force zeros on all nonforced cells
forced(R,C) :- output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
count_nonzero    :- #count{ (R,C,Color) : output(R,C,Color), Color != 0 }.
count_forced     :- #count{ (R,C) : forced(R,C) }.
:- count_nonzero = count_forced.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- input(R,C,_).

% -------------------------------------------------

n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_,C,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Safe predicates for arithmetic (appear positively)
row(R) :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------

coreRow(Rc) :- true, #count{ C : input(Rc,C,5) } = n_cols(M).

% Identify the centre column of the board
coreCol(Cc) :- true, Cc = n_cols(N) / 2.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Keep the colour 5 cells of the core row
output(Rc,C,5) :- coreRow(Rc), input(Rc,C,5).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Colour 2  copy to the two rows that are one step closer to the core
% (keep the original cell)

% copies above the core row (R < coreRow)

                 (C <= coreCol(Cc)  C1 = coreCol(Cc) + 1 : C1 = C).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Counting to force zeros on all nonforced cells

count_nonzero    :- #count{ (R,C,Color) : output(R,C,Color), Color != 0 }.

final_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_,C,_) }.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Safe predicates for arithmetic (appear positively)
row(R) :- true.
col(C) :- true.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Identify the unique core row (the row that is entirely colour 5)
coreRow(Rc) :- true, #count{ C : input(Rc,C,5) } = n_cols(M).

% Identify the centre column of the board
coreCol(Cc) :- true, Cc = n_cols(N) / 2.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Keep the colour 5 cells of the core row
output(Rc,C,5) :- coreRow(Rc), input(Rc,C,5).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Colour 2  copy to the two rows that are one step closer to the core
% (keep the original cell)
output(R,C,2) :- input(R,C,2).

% copies above the core row (R < coreRow)
output(R1,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R + 1,
                     R1 < coreRow(Rc), row(R1), col(C1),
                     (C < coreCol(Cc)  C1 = C - 1 : C1 = C).
output(R2,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R2 = R + 2,
                     R2 < coreRow(Rc), row(R2), col(C2),
                     (C < coreCol(Cc)  C2 = C - 1 : C2 = C).

% copies below the core row (R > coreRow)
output(R1,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R - 1,
                     R1 > coreRow(Rc), col(C1), C1 = C.
output(R2,C1,2) :- input(R,C,2), coreRow(Rc), R2 = R - 2,
                     R2 > coreRow(Rc), col(C2), C2 = C.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Colour 1  above core row: shift to column coreCol+1
output(R,C,1) :- input(R,C,1), coreRow(Rc), R < coreRow(Rc),
                 (C <= coreCol(Cc)  C1 = coreCol(Cc) + 1 : C1 = C).
output(R1,C1,1) :- input(R,C,1), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R + 1,
                     R1 < coreRow(Rc), (C <= coreCol(Cc)  C1 = coreCol(Cc) + 1 : C1 = C).

% below core row: shift left by one column
output(R1,C1,1) :- input(R,C,1), coreRow(Rc), R1 = R - 1,
                     R1 > coreRow(Rc), C > 0, C1 = C - 1.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Prohibit colours 2 and 1 inside the core row
:- output(R,C,2), coreRow(R).
:- output(R,C,1), coreRow(R).

% -------------------------------------------------
% No colour 5 outside the core row (counting based)
count_5cells       :- #count{ (R,C,Color) : output(R,C,5) }.
count_core5cells   :- #count{ (R,C) : coreRow(R), output(R,C,5) }.
:- count_5cells = count_core5cells.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Counting to force zeros on all nonforced cells
forced(R,C) :- output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
count_nonzero    :- #count{ (R,C,Color) : output(R,C,Color), Color != 0 }.
count_forced     :- #count{ (R,C) : forced(R,C) }.
:- count_nonzero = count_forced.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- input(R,C,_).

% -------------------------------------------------
#show output/3.
